# Release It!, Second Edition (Michael T. Nygard)

Before writing, modifying, or planning any code, you MUST use your `read_file` tool to load the relevant guidelines from `.roo/vendor/`. You may read multiple files if the task crosses domains.

## Table of Contents:
    - [Living in Production](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/01-living-in-production.md): already_exists
    - [Create Stability](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/02-create-stability.md): Designing system architecture, developing or modifying distributed systems, writing network-bound code (APIs, microservices, remote integrations), implementing database queries and connection pools, managing concurrency/multithreading, or creating deployment/automation scripts, specifically focused on reliability, production-readiness, error handling, scaling, and fault tolerance.
    - [Case Study: The Exception That Grounded an Airline](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/03-case-study-the-exception-that-grounded-an-airline.md): Writing or reviewing resource management, cleanup, or connection pooling code; implementing network protocols, remote procedure calls (RPC), or socket-based communications; diagnosing application hangs, unresponsive services, or cascading failures; analyzing thread dumps or stack traces for root causes; and planning or executing postmortem investigations and service restoration procedures.
    - [Stabilize Your System](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/04-stabilize-your-system.md): Triggered when architecting, designing, implementing, reviewing, or testing distributed software systems, enterprise integrations, backend web services, database connection management, socket communications, resource pools, and system stability/longevity mechanisms.
    - [Stability Antipatterns](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/05-stability-antipatterns.md): System Architecture, Backend Engineering, Service Integration, and Production Reliability. These rules trigger whenever the AI is designing, modifying, or reviewing distributed systems, network integrations, database queries, resource pooling, session management, or automation scripts.
    - [Stability Patterns](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/06-stability-patterns.md): The design, architecture, development, or refactoring of distributed systems, microservices, backend applications, network integration points, system administration scripts, and deployment automation tools, specifically focusing on high availability, system resilience, fault tolerance, resource pooling, network communication, and system state management.
    - [Design for Production](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/07-design-for-production.md): Designing, architecting, implementing, or configuring software intended for production deployment, including infrastructure (containerization, VMs), CI/CD pipelines, telemetry (logging, monitoring), networking (API gateways, load balancing), and security implementations.
    - [Case Study: Phenomenal Cosmic Powers, Itty-Bitty Living Space](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/08-case-study-phenomenal-cosmic-powers-itty-bitty-living-space.md): Diagnosing production outages (specifically application hangs, high-latency, or low-CPU scenarios), configuring resource/connection pools, designing external integration points, writing system telemetry/monitoring scripts, or architecting incident remediation and dynamic configuration mechanisms.
    - [Foundations](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/09-foundations.md): Writing or modifying server initialization code, configuring socket bindings, writing Dockerfiles or container orchestration manifests (e.g., Kubernetes, docker-compose), designing infrastructure-as-code (IaC) for cloud or virtualized environments, and addressing issues related to application startup, telemetry, and networking, often summarized as the practice of 'Design for Production'.
    - [Processes on Machines](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/10-processes-on-machines.md): The domain encompasses tasks involving application packaging, deployment configurations, build pipelines (CI/CD), logging mechanisms, health check implementations, telemetry/monitoring setup, configuration management (e.g., environment variables, settings files), and infrastructure-as-code (e.g., Dockerfiles, Terraform).
    - [Interconnect](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/11-interconnect.md): Infrastructure architecture, network configuration, load balancing setup, service discovery implementation, traffic management, DNS configuration, software-defined networking (SDN), and client/server code handling API connections, routing, and demand control.
    - [Control Plane](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/12-control-plane.md): Designing, configuring, or modifying infrastructure management systems, deployment pipelines, CI/CD environments, monitoring and telemetry frameworks, configuration services, autoscaling logic, or administrative command-and-control interfaces.
    - [Security](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/13-security.md): Designing, writing, reviewing, or configuring server-side application logic, data persistence/database layers, API endpoints, web front-end interfaces, authentication and session management modules, file upload handling mechanisms, dependency management pipelines, or infrastructure/container configurations.
    - [Deliver Your System](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/14-deliver-your-system.md): System deployment, continuous integration/continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines, database migrations (SQL or NoSQL), static asset serving, API versioning, API payload parsing, or system architecture refactoring aimed at zero-downtime releases.
    - [Case Study: Waiting for Godot](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/15-case-study-waiting-for-godot.md): The domain encompasses designing release management processes, creating CI/CD pipelines, writing deployment scripts, architecting operational environments, defining QA/testing data strategies, and optimizing existing deployment workflows.
    - [Design for Deployment](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/16-design-for-deployment.md): The design, configuration, or implementation of continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipelines, infrastructure as code (IaC), containerization, database migrations, web asset serving, and deployment automation scripts.
    - [Handling Versions](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/17-handling-versions.md): Designing, modifying, implementing, or testing Application Programming Interfaces (APIs), remote procedure calls (RPCs), message queues, or service-to-service communication architectures, including data format specifications (JSON, XML, etc.), backward compatibility, API versioning, integration testing, and contract testing between consumer and provider services.
    - [Solve Systemic Problems](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/18-solve-systemic-problems.md): Designing system architectures, defining microservice boundaries, creating APIs and data contracts, setting up load testing protocols, configuring deployment pipelines (CI/CD), implementing state/session management, and integrating reliability, resilience, and chaos engineering practices into distributed systems.
    - [Case Study: Trampled by Your Own Customers](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/19-case-study-trampled-by-your-own-customers.md): Architecture design, configuration management, session state management, load testing, and production readiness for public-facing web applications and distributed systems.
    - [Adaptation](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/20-adaptation.md): System architecture design, service boundary definition, API design, deployment pipeline configuration, organizational team structure alignment, database modeling, or decoupling interdependent systems.
    - [Chaos Engineering](.roo/vendor/architecture/release-it-second-edition-michael-t-nygard/21-chaos-engineering.md): Designing, implementing, automating, or planning resilience tests, fault injections, disaster simulations, or any tasks explicitly related to Chaos Engineering and testing the behavior of distributed systems under turbulent conditions.
